description: Facing limper(s): isolate-raise / over-limp(call) / fold, by limper count + hero position. Iso ranges value-skewed; over-limp reserved for speculative hands in multiway. Live-specific.

entries.rationale: First to act with the whole table behind, isolate only hands that dominate a weak limper AND fare well if a player behind wakes up (99+, AJs+, AQo+); the medium pairs and suited Broadways/connectors over-limp to see a cheap multiway flop where implied odds beat thin isolation from out of position (Upswing vs-limpers; doc 01 §9).

One off EP: widen the iso slightly (88+, suited Broadways, AJo/KQo) as fewer players remain behind, while small pairs and suited connectors over-limp for set/straight equity in a likely multiway pot (Upswing vs-limpers; doc 01 §9).

actions.combos: 77+, A9s+, KTs+, QTs+, JTs, ATo+, KJo+, A5s

entries.rationale: Mirror the CO iso one seat earlier and marginally tighter: value-skewed isolation (77+, suited aces/Broadways, ATo+/KJo+) to play a heads-up pot in position against a capped limper, with the small pairs and suited connectors over-limping for cheap multiway equity (Upswing vs-limpers; doc 01 §9).

actions.combos: 66+, A9s+, KTs+, QTs+, JTs, ATo+, KJo+, A5s

entries.rationale: Never limp behind — isolate to play heads-up in position with hands that dominate a weak limping range (66+, suited aces/Broadways, ATo+), sizing up to punish the dead money; the small pairs and suited connectors instead over-limp, taking cheap multiway flops where their set/straight implied odds beat trying to isolate (doc 01 §9).

actions.combos: 66+, A9s+, KTs+, QTs+, JTs, ATo+, KJo+, A5s

entries.rationale: From the small blind you close only to the big blind but play every street out of position, so isolate a value-heavy range that wants a heads-up pot (66+, suited aces/Broadways, ATo+); complete (over-limp) with speculative hands rather than fold, since the discounted 0.5bb price prices in set/straight draws even out of position (Upswing vs-limpers; doc 01 §9).

The Button is the best seat to isolate from, so you widen the iso considerably (55+, suited gappers, offsuit Broadways) because guaranteed position lets you realize equity against a capped limper; the tiny pairs and weak suited hands over-limp instead, keeping the pot small and multiway where their implied odds — not raw equity — pay off (doc 01 §9).

Two limpers means the pot likely stays multiway even after a raise, so tighten to hands that hold up against several callers (88+, suited aces/Broadways) and size to 4bb+2 (6bb); speculative pairs and connectors over-limp, since 3-way-plus pots reward set-mining and drawing over thin isolation (Upswing vs-limpers; doc 01 §9, §11).

Out of position against two limpers, isolate only a tight value core (88+, suited Broadways, AJo+) and complete the rest, leaning on the cheap SB price to set-mine and draw in a guaranteed-multiway pot rather than bloating it out of position with marginal iso hands (Upswing vs-limpers; doc 01 §9, §11).

With two limpers the pot likely goes multiway even after a raise, so you tighten the isolation to hands that fare well against several callers (77+, suited aces/Broadways) and size larger (4bb + 1bb per limper); more speculative pairs and connectors shift to over-limping, since 3-way-plus pots reward set-mining and drawing over thin isolation (doc 01 §9, §11).
